abstract.text: A 3D particle emitter.

inlineContent.text: 3D particle node used to create a variety of particle systems and effects.
features an emitter that generates some number of particles at a given rate.
to configure particle appearance and behavior. Alternatively, you can add a
which will be applied to all particles.
This object emits the following signals:

abstract.text: If
, particles use the parent node’s coordinate space (known as local coordinates). This will cause particles to move and rotate along the
node (and its parents) when it is moved or rotated. If
, particles use global coordinates; they will not move or rotate along the
node (and its parents) when it is moved or rotated.
Returns the axis-aligned bounding box that contains all the particles that are active in the current frame.
that is drawn for the second draw pass.
Causes all the particles in this node to interpolate towards the end of their lifetime.
that is drawn for the third draw pass.
Variant objects box various Godot Objects, you create them with one of the
constructors, and you can retrieve the contents using the various extension
constructors that are declared on the various types that are wrapped.
Base node for geometry-based visual instances.
Types that conform to VariantStorable can be stored in a Variant and can be extracted
back out of a Variant.

The particle system’s frame rate is fixed to a value. For example, changing the value to 2 will make the particles render at 2 frames per second. Note this does not slow down the simulation of the particle system itself.
